title: China's coal and energy strategy
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
In the autumn of 2021, a wave of blackouts swept across China's industrial northeast, silencing factories, disrupting ports, and even cutting power to residential neighbourhoods - an unprecedented event in a country known for its tight control over infrastructure and energy systems. The episode marked more than just a temporary power crisis; it was a moment of reckoning that would reshape the foundations of China's coal and energy strategy in the years to follow. The immediate causes of the 2021 crisis were multifaceted.
